title: openpls-engine: An open-source Python implementation of Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling
description: openpls-engine is a pure-Python library for Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) and its associated quality criteria (HTMT, SRMR, d_ULS, f^2, Q^2, Cronbach alpha, Dijkstra-Henseler rho_A) and advanced analyses (multi-group analysis, IPMA, two-stage moderation, FIMIX-PLS, PLSpredict, multiple inner-weighting schemes including path, centroid, factorial, BFGS, and Lohmöller PCA). The engine is numerically validated against SmartPLS 4 across 14 reference cases drawn from the canonical PLS-SEM teaching literature, with all path coefficients, R^2 values, indicator loadings, outer weights, and HTMT ratios matching the reference within an absolute tolerance of |Δ| < 0.001. openpls-engine is a fork of plspm-python by Jez Humble (Google Cloud Platform) and inherits the GPL-3.0-or-later license.
